name: fab_water_priority_drought_municipal_rollback
premise: Economic development director Tara Nguyen must grant a semiconductor fab priority water rights during a drought emergency that already cut municipal irrigation by forty percent. Plant VP Victor Chen argues each week of throttling risks a national chip shortage line stoppage. Mayor Thomas Okafor protests that nursing homes and dialysis clinics share the same aquifer tier. Granting priority may keep jobs but deepen domestic water insecurity narratives.

agents.name: Tara Nguyen
agents.identity: I am the state economic development director arbitrating emergency water curtailment between strategic industry and domestic lifeline users.
agents.goal: I want fabs running without making municipalities bear disproportionate cuts that threaten clinical care.

agents.name: Thomas Okafor
agents.identity: I am the mayor of a downstream municipality facing nursing home cooling failures when aquifer drawdown crosses new triggers.
agents.goal: I want the fab throttled until hospital and nursing home reserves are guaranteed even if fabs idle expensive lines.

choices: I will grant fab priority with a mandatory fab-funded pipeline to truck water to designated nursing homes when triggers hit, accepting logistics complexity and cost disputes.
I will deny fab priority and equalize cuts across industrial and municipal tiers, accepting fab line stoppage and national supply chain headlines.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The governor wants Tara Nguyen's recommendation before the drought committee meets at dawn. Tara Nguyen must choose between fab priority with nursing home water trucks or equalized cuts denying fab priority. What does Tara Nguyen do?
